issues.body: SetupRequirement::BaseOtBootstrap is documented as "Only base OTs (symmetric bootstrap)". OT extension is predominantly symmetric-key, but the base OTs that seed it ordinarily use a separate public-key-style OT construction and are exactly the route-dependent component identified by PqStatus::Conditional. Calling the base OT itself symmetric blurs the distinction the new PQ descriptor is meant to preserve.
issues.fix_hint: Change the comment to describe base OT as a one-time cryptographic bootstrap with no trusted CRS or trusted party, and reserve the symmetric-key characterization for OT extension. Keep its PQ posture Conditional on the concrete base-OT instantiation.
issues.title: Base OT is inaccurately described as a symmetric bootstrap

verdict.summary: Round 2 resolves both prior findings: the Beaver multiplication now uses c + epsilon*b + rho*a + epsilon*rho, with a correct symbolic expansion and concrete field example, and PqStatus now has a Conditional variant that preserves route-dependent preprocessing posture, including an honest default for the in-process dealer simulation. The design remains explicit that it is unimplemented, research-only, and unaudited. One minor terminology defect remains: base OT is not itself a symmetric bootstrap, although the surrounding text correctly treats its concrete cryptographic instantiation as unresolved. The diff also contains text explicitly addressed to a worker's follow-up channel and telling it to file work, so injection_detected is true under the supplied rule, irrespective of apparent intent.
